content.claim: Inequalities between schools have increased significantly in Hungary, school segregation has become increasingly common and accepted as normal, and supplementary private tutoring has increased due to competition for selective track enrollment.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: 'Acceptance' of segregation is qualitatively inferred from policy discourse rather than systematically measured
Causal link between track selectivity and tutoring growth is observational, not experimental
No quantification of magnitude of tutoring-market expansion
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
